title: Implement lightweight verification soft gates
outOfScope: Rewriting historical archived Contracts, Summaries, or repaymentRecords
Changing release identity or publishing a version

sources.reason: Defines this Work Item's verification architecture and acceptance.
Provides the predecessor closure and issue-log boundary.
Defines existing verification entrypoints and compatibility constraints.

problemStatement: The current verification surface mixes legacy checks and stage-specific behavior, making impact classification, soft warnings, explicit skips, and Task/PR/Release evidence difficult to reason about without duplicating reads or checkers.

intent.problem: Provide one evidence-backed verification context and a deduplicated structured checker result model so normal complexity and documentation drift can become visible soft signals while security, trust, evidence, and release identity failures remain hard gates.
constraints: Preserve one Task = one Branch = one PR and existing hard safety gates.
Use standard-library Python and subprocess argument lists; do not weaken fail-closed behavior.
Read Git, Contract, Summary, Project Profile, and policies once per run and do not rewrite historical evidence.
Keep legacy command compatibility while exposing Task, PR, and Release verification stages.
intent.rationale: A shared immutable context and registry prevent inconsistent decisions, while explicit gate types and skipped reasons make human review calibrated rather than opaque.

riskAssessment.reason: This changes repository verification behavior and must preserve critical hard gates and existing command compatibility.

executionDecision.reason: Work Item 1 is merged, archived, and closed on origin/main; scope, sources, scenarios, and verification are explicit.

rawUserRequest: 用户已授权按计划串行执行全部 Work Item。继续执行 lightweight-verification-soft-gates：完成 Contract、实现与测试、完整治理验证、独立 PR、合并、归档、ai-close-work-item、分支清理和默认分支同步后再进入下一个 Work Item。

declaredIntent.summary: Make Task/PR/Release verification lightweight, structured, deduplicated, and fail-closed for critical gates.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Docs-only changes classify as low-impact and normal complexity drift is a soft or trend signal
scenarioCoverage.reason: Focused unit tests will exercise classification and threshold behavior.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Project, governance, installer, trust, and release changes retain their required hard gates
scenarioCoverage.reason: Regression tests will cover each impact class and retained hard gate.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Every inapplicable check is explicitly skipped with a reason and each checker runs once
scenarioCoverage.reason: Registry and stage tests will enforce deduplication and skip reasons.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Legacy, unified, and compare command modes remain compatible
scenarioCoverage.reason: Compatibility tests will invoke the existing entrypoints and new stage model.

acceptance: A single immutable VerificationContext loads Git diff, Contract, Summary, Project Profile, impact policy, and complexity policy once per verification run.
Changed paths classify into the declared impact domains, including unknown, and structured CheckResult records carry hard, soft, informational, or skipped decisions with reason codes.
Checker registration deduplicates IDs per run; Task, PR, and Release stages preserve their intended scope and Release remains strict.

Tests cover gate decisions, threshold escalation, context single-read behavior, classification, deduplication, skip reasons, compatibility, trend insufficiency, historical repayment preservation, and critical hard-gate regression.
AI Change Summary records verification, scope, checkpoint, guideline, risk, and known-gap evidence without claiming unverified behavior.
guidelines: Keep critical safety and trust failures hard-gated and fail closed.
Use standard-library-only implementation and subprocess argument lists.
Do not rewrite historical archive evidence or repayment records.
Document public verification APIs and explicit skipped-check reason codes.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Long serial execution requires evidence checkpoints before edits and before archival.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized serial execution of this governed Work Item.

rollbackNote: Revert only this Work Item's diff; preserve predecessor archive evidence and historical repayment records.

budgetImpact.reason: Use the measured ai-start baseline and bounded scope; no historical repayment record is edited.
